I suffered from a stroke after an artery clotted and cut off blood supply to my brain, causing me to lose consciousness for several hours before regaining it gradually.

Now I'm back at work with some residual weakness on one side of my body. The doctor says as long as the blockage doesn't recur, there’s hope for a full recovery over time - but we have no way to guarantee that will happen or how quickly I might get it. It certainly feels strange not being able to move parts smoothly and independently like before – yet life has its own pace of healing as well; all those seemingly small moments add up into something substantial, even if one is just watching time slip by unnoticed most days while the mind tries hard work on itself.
